ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea; closest match is canned iced tea (FOODON_03301099). Lemon-flavoured variant not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers RTD tea beverages broadly; no specific Fuze Tea lemon variant code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for canned lemon iced tea beverage; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 300 ml can of Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea, as sold at Wingstop and similar fast-food outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Fuze Tea Lemon Tea — Nutrition Facts (USDA Branded Food / Coca-Cola product label data)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Foods; corroborated by Coca-Cola product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Branded Foods contains Fuze Tea Lemon Tea entries (e.g., FDC ID 2049832 and related entries). Values are per 100 ml (approximately equal to per 100 g for this beverage given density ~1.0 g/ml). Serving size adapted to 300 ml can. Nutrient values reflect the standard Fuze Tea Lemon Tea formulation: approximately 19–20 kcal per 100 ml, ~5 g sugar per 100 ml, negligible protein and fat, and trace sodium. These values are consistent across multiple USDA Branded Food entries for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea / lemon tea beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea specifically. GI of 28 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea / lemon-flavoured tea beverages (analogous RTD tea with similar sugar content ~5 g/100 ml). Sweetened tea beverages with moderate sugar content typically fall in the GI range 25–35 (low category). GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 14.4) / 100 = 4.0; rounded to 4.0 g per 300 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~30 mg per 300 ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea contains approximately 30 mg sodium per 300 ml serving, which is low and unlikely to meaningfully contribute to blood pressure elevation; however, individuals with hypertension should remain mindful of total daily sugar intake from sweetened beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~14.4 g per 300 ml can) contributing to postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 300 ml can provides approximately 14.4 g of sugar with a low GI (~28), resulting in a moderate glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of sweetened RTD tea beverages and account for the sugar content within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea contains no fat, no saturated fat, and minimal sodium, making it low risk from a lipid and blood pressure perspective; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, and moderation is advised for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea (canned, 300 ml) is a ready-to-drink sweetened iced lemon tea beverage produced by The Coca-Cola Company under the Fuze Tea brand. It is widely available in Singapore, including as a beverage option at fast-food chains such as Wingstop. The drink is made from water, sugar, black tea extract, lemon juice concentrate, citric acid, natural flavourings, and ascorbic acid (vitamin C). It has a light, refreshing taste with a mild tea flavour and a pleasant lemon tartness, balanced by moderate sweetness. The texture is thin and watery, typical of a canned RTD tea. Each 300 ml can provides approximately 57 kcal, 14.4 g of carbohydrate (all as sugar), negligible protein, and no fat. Sodium content is low at approximately 30 mg per can. The beverage has a low glycemic index (estimated ~28) but delivers a moderate glycemic load per serving due to its sugar content. It is classified as ultra-processed. Clinically, it is low risk for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible fat and sodium, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for its sugar content within their daily carbohydrate allowance. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal diets. It is gluten-free and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA Branded Foods entries for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea, which represent the closest approved-source match for this commercially produced beverage. The formulation sold in Singapore may differ slightly from the US formulation in sugar content or additives, but the values are consistent with the product label profile. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar sweetened RTD tea beverages, as no direct GI measurement for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea was found in the approved GI database.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero — Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero — this beverage contains no fat by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this beverage. 5. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA Branded Foods source entries for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 13. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 15. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 16. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 17. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 18. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 19. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 20. GI set to 28 (low category) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened RTD lemon tea beverages; no direct GI measurement found for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea. GL per 300 ml serving = (28 × 14.4) / 100 = 4.0 g. 21. The input cuisine field was 'Fried Chicken' (reflecting the restaurant context at Wingstop); this has been corrected to 'Beverages' as the canonical cuisine classification for this product. 22. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this product is sold in Singapore and the Singaporean multilingual naming rule applies. 23. The product is sold at Wingstop Singapore locations (City Square Mall and Funan) per the input field survey data.
